title: Combine metadata filters with vector search safely
description: Use the engine's native pre-filtering so a metadata predicate narrows candidates before ANN, not after.
body: Use when a query needs both a metadata filter and nearest-neighbour search. Do not run the ANN search first and filter the results afterward; post-filtering can leave far fewer than k matches because the filtered-out vectors were among the nearest. Actions: use the engine's index-aware pre-filtering (Qdrant payload filters, Milvus boolean expressions, pgvector with a partial index or filtered scan) so the filter constrains the candidate set the index searches. Verify the query returns a full k results that all satisfy the filter. Validation signals include k matching results returned and the filter applied within the index path. Risk boundary: naive post-filtering silently degrades result count and quality, so confirm the engine pushes the predicate into the search rather than applying it after top-k.
